summary:
The majority of dental literature supports an approximate mesiodistal width-to-height ratio for maxillary canines around 8:10. This aligns well with general standards used by dentists despite not being explicitly cited everywhere; no contradictory data was found challenging this common practice.

The summary highlights two main aspects from Agent1’s reasoning regarding the mesiodistal width-to-height ratio of maxillary canines based primarily on supporting documentation indicating it typically ranges near 8:10 while noting its widespread acceptance among practitioners.
